server.h(3) Library Functions Manual server.h(3)

NAME

server.h - Server-side H.265 decoding pipeline for multi-client streams.

SYNOPSIS

Typedefs


typedef struct h265_server_client h265_server_client_t

Functions


h265_server_context_t * h265_server_context_create (void)
void h265_server_context_destroy (h265_server_context_t *ctx)
h265_server_client_t * h265_server_get_client_decoder (h265_server_context_t *ctx, uint32_t client_id)
asciichat_error_t h265_server_decode_and_convert (h265_server_client_t *client, const uint8_t *h265_packet, size_t packet_size, uint8_t *output_rgba, uint16_t *output_width, uint16_t *output_height, size_t *output_size)
void h265_server_remove_client (h265_server_context_t *ctx, uint32_t client_id)
void h265_server_client_get_stats (h265_server_client_t *client, uint64_t *total_frames, uint64_t *keyframes, uint16_t *last_width, uint16_t *last_height)

Detailed Description

Server-side H.265 decoding pipeline for multi-client streams.

The server maintains a separate H.265 decoder for each connected client. When a client sends an H.265-encoded frame, the server:

1.
Identifies the decoder for that client
2.
Decodes the H.265 frame to ASCII art
3.
Converts ASCII art to RGBA pixels for compositing
4.
Adds the RGBA frame to the compositing pipeline

Definition in file src/session/h265/server.h.

Typedef Documentation

typedef struct h265_server_client h265_server_client_t

Function Documentation

void h265_server_client_get_stats (h265_server_client_t * client, uint64_t * total_frames, uint64_t * keyframes, uint16_t * last_width, uint16_t * last_height)

Get statistics for a client's decoder

Parameters

client Client decoder
total_frames Output: total frames decoded
keyframes Output: total keyframes decoded
last_width Output: width of last decoded frame
last_height Output: height of last decoded frame

Definition at line 167 of file src/session/h265/server.c.

173   {
174     if (!client) return;
175 
176     h265_client_decoder_t *client_dec = (h265_client_decoder_t *)client;
177     if (!client_dec->decoder) return;
178 
179     h265_decoder_get_stats(client_dec->decoder, total_frames, keyframes, last_width, last_height);
180 }

h265_server_context_t * h265_server_context_create (void
  )


Create a server-side H.265 decoding context Manages decoders for
    all connected clients




Returns


Context handle, or NULL on error


Definition at line 27 of file
    src/session/h265/server.c.


27                                                         {
28     h265_server_context_t *ctx = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(h265_server_context_t), h265_server_context_t *);
29     if (!ctx) {
30         SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate server context");
31         return NULL;
32     }
33 
34     for (int i = 0; i < MAX_CLIENTS; i++) {
35         ctx->clients[i].in_use = false;
36     }
37     ctx->active_client_count = 0;
38 
39     log_info("H.265 server context created (max %d clients)", MAX_CLIENTS);
40     return ctx;
41 }






References h265_server_context::active_client_count,
    h265_server_context::clients, ERROR_MEMORY,
    h265_client_decoder_t::in_use, log_info, MAX_CLIENTS,
    SAFE_CALLOC, and SET_ERRNO.

void
  h265_server_context_destroy (h265_server_context_t * ctx)


Destroy the server context and all client decoders




Parameters


ctx Context to destroy (may be NULL)


Definition at line 43 of file
    src/session/h265/server.c.


43                                                              {
44     if (!ctx) return;
45 
46     for (int i = 0; i < MAX_CLIENTS; i++) {
47         if (ctx->clients[i].in_use && ctx->clients[i].decoder) {
48             h265_decoder_destroy(ctx->clients[i].decoder);
49         }
50     }
51     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
52 }

    h265_client_decoder_t::in_use, MAX_CLIENTS, and

Referenced by server_main().






asciichat_error_t h265_server_decode_and_convert
  (h265_server_client_t * client, const uint8_t * h265_packet,
  size_t packet_size, uint8_t * output_rgba, uint16_t *
  output_width, uint16_t * output_height, size_t * output_size)


Decode an H.265 frame from a client and convert to RGBA




Parameters


client Client decoder handle


h265_packet H.265 encoded packet data (with header)


packet_size Size of packet


output_rgba Output buffer for RGBA pixel data


output_width Output: frame width in pixels


output_height Output: frame height in pixels


output_size Input: buffer size, Output: actual RGBA size
  (width*height*4)


Returns


Error code (ASCIICHAT_OK on success)


RGBA conversion: Each ASCII character (0-255) becomes a grayscale
    pixel where pixel = (ascii_val, ascii_val, ascii_val, 255)


Definition at line 93 of file
    src/session/h265/server.c.


101   {
102     if (!client || !h265_packet || !output_rgba || !output_width || !output_height || !output_size) {
103         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Invalid arguments to decode_and_convert");
104     }
105 
106     h265_client_decoder_t *client_dec = (h265_client_decoder_t *)client;
107     if (!client_dec->decoder || !client_dec->in_use) {
108         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Invalid client decoder state");
109     }
110 
111     uint8_t ascii_buf[256 * 64];
112     size_t ascii_size = sizeof(ascii_buf);
113 
114     asciichat_error_t result = h265_decode(
115         client_dec->decoder,
116         h265_packet,
117         packet_size,
118         output_width,
119         output_height,
120         ascii_buf,
121         &ascii_size
122     );
123 
124     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
125         return result;
126     }
127 
128     size_t required_rgba_size = (size_t)(*output_width) * (*output_height) * 4;
129     if (*output_size < required_rgba_size) {
130         *output_size = required_rgba_size;
131         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_SIZE, "Output buffer too small: need %zu, have %zu",
132                         required_rgba_size, *output_size);
133     }
134 
135     for (size_t i = 0; i < ascii_size && i < (size_t)(*output_width) * (*output_height); i++) {
136         uint8_t val = ascii_buf[i];
137         output_rgba[i * 4 + 0] = val;
138         output_rgba[i * 4 + 1] = val;
139         output_rgba[i * 4 + 2] = val;
140         output_rgba[i * 4 + 3] = 255;
141     }
142 
143     *output_size = required_rgba_size;
144     client_dec->last_width = *output_width;
145     client_dec->last_height = *output_height;
146 
147     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
148 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    h265_client_decoder_t::decoder, ERROR_INTERNAL,
    ERROR_NETWORK_SIZE, h265_decode(),
    h265_client_decoder_t::in_use,
    h265_client_decoder_t::last_height,
    h265_client_decoder_t::last_width, and SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by handle_image_frame_h265_packet().






h265_server_client_t * h265_server_get_client_decoder
  (h265_server_context_t * ctx, uint32_t client_id)


Get or create a client decoder within the server context Each
    client gets its own H.265 decoder instance




Parameters


ctx Server context


client_id Unique client identifier


Returns


Client decoder handle, or NULL on error


Definition at line 54 of file
    src/session/h265/server.c.


57   {
58     if (!ctx) {
59         SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Invalid server context");
60         return NULL;
61     }
62 
63     for (int i = 0; i < MAX_CLIENTS; i++) {
64         if (ctx->clients[i].in_use && ctx->clients[i].client_id == client_id) {
65             return (h265_server_client_t *)&ctx->clients[i];
66         }
67     }
68 
69     for (int i = 0; i < MAX_CLIENTS; i++) {
70         if (!ctx->clients[i].in_use) {
71             h265_decoder_t *decoder = h265_decoder_create();
72             if (!decoder) {
73                 SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEDIA_INIT, "Failed to create decoder for client %u", client_id);
74                 return NULL;
75             }
76 
77             ctx->clients[i].client_id = client_id;
78             ctx->clients[i].decoder = decoder;
79             ctx->clients[i].in_use = true;
80             ctx->clients[i].last_width = 0;
81             ctx->clients[i].last_height = 0;
82             ctx->active_client_count++;
83 
84             log_debug("Created H.265 decoder for client %u", client_id);
85             return (h265_server_client_t *)&ctx->clients[i];
86         }
87     }
88 
89     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "No free decoder slots (max %d clients)", MAX_CLIENTS);
90     return NULL;
91 }






References h265_server_context::active_client_count,
    h265_client_decoder_t::client_id,
    h265_server_context::clients, h265_client_decoder_t::decoder,
    ERROR_INTERNAL, ERROR_MEDIA_INIT,
    h265_decoder_create(), h265_client_decoder_t::in_use,
    h265_client_decoder_t::last_height,
    h265_client_decoder_t::last_width, log_debug,
    MAX_CLIENTS, and SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by handle_image_frame_h265_packet().






void
  h265_server_remove_client (h265_server_context_t * ctx, uint32_t
  client_id)


Remove a client decoder (when client disconnects)




Parameters


ctx Server context


client_id Client identifier


Definition at line 150 of file
    src/session/h265/server.c.


150                                                                                {
151     if (!ctx) return;
152 
153     for (int i = 0; i < MAX_CLIENTS; i++) {
154         if (ctx->clients[i].in_use && ctx->clients[i].client_id == client_id) {
155             if (ctx->clients[i].decoder) {
156                 h265_decoder_destroy(ctx->clients[i].decoder);
157                 ctx->clients[i].decoder = NULL;
158             }
159             ctx->clients[i].in_use = false;
160             ctx->active_client_count--;
161             log_debug("Removed H.265 decoder for client %u", client_id);
162             return;
163         }
164     }
165 }






References h265_server_context::active_client_count,
    h265_client_decoder_t::client_id,
    h265_server_context::clients, h265_client_decoder_t::decoder,
    h265_decoder_destroy(), h265_client_decoder_t::in_use,
    log_debug, and MAX_CLIENTS.
